entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Cajanus cajan (L.) Millsp., the crop studied for dwarfing genes

entities.name: EXCESSIVE VEGETATIVE GROWTH
entities.description: A characteristic of pigeonpea cultivars that makes them unsuitable for certain management practices

entities.name: FOLIAR INSECTICIDE APPLICATION
entities.description: A crop management practice hindered by excessive vegetative growth

entities.name: MECHANIZED FIELD PRODUCTION
entities.description: A crop management practice hindered by excessive vegetative growth

entities.name: ICRISAT CENTER IN INDIA
entities.description: The location where the research on pigeonpea dwarfing genes was conducted

entities.name: DWARF PHENOTYPE
entities.description: A morphological characteristic observed in pigeonpea lines D6, PD1, and PBNA

entities.name: MORPHOLOGICAL TRAITS
entities.description: Physical characteristics used to characterize dwarf pigeonpea phenotypes

entities.name: INHERITANCE PATTERNS
entities.description: The genetic patterns by which dwarfing genes are passed to offspring

entities.name: DWARFING GENES
entities.description: Genes responsible for the dwarf phenotype in pigeonpea

entities.name: CROSSES
entities.description: Breeding method involving dwarf lines and tall cultivars to study inheritance

entities.name: TESTCROSSES
entities.description: Breeding method used to evaluate F3 progenies and parents

entities.name: CHI-SQUARE ANALYSES
entities.description: Statistical method used to determine the genetic control of the dwarf phenotype

entities.name: SINGLE RECESSIVE GENE
entities.description: The genetic unit controlling the dwarf phenotype in a homozygous state

entities.name: HOMOZYGOUS STATE
entities.description: The genetic condition required for the expression of the recessive dwarf gene

entities.name: ALLELES
entities.description: Specific versions of the dwarfing gene, such as t3t3

entities.name: MULTIPLE ALLELIC SERIES
entities.description: A series of different alleles for the same gene controlling dwarfness
